title: Phase 2 tests: tools — shell execution [CC parity]
description:
## Goal

Implement Rust tests covering the behavioral spec produced by Phase 1 (#526). Pin down every contract listed in that spec with at least one test case.

Parent audit: #523.

## Blocked on

- Phase 1 spec issue #526. Do not start until that body contains the markdown spec.

## Where the tests live

- `src/tools/bash/mod.rs` — tests for BackgroundShellManager spawn/output/kill lifecycle
- `src/tools/bash/policy.rs` — tests for env scrubbing + blocked-pattern detection
- `src/tools/bash/kill.rs` — tests for kill-by-shell_id (use a `sleep 30` child)
- `tests/bash_integration.rs` (new) — full background-shell flow

## Test plan skeleton

- Read the spec at #526.
- For each behavior bullet: add a `#[test]` (or `#[tokio::test]`) named after the behavior.
- For pure functions: `#[cfg(test)] mod tests` colocated in the same source file.
- For multi-module / cross-process / SSE / HTTP: `tests/<subsystem>_integration.rs`.
- Use `tempfile::TempDir` for filesystem isolation.
- Use `wiremock` (already in deps) for HTTP fakes; avoid network IO.
- Property tests (`proptest`) for input-parsers where useful.

## Notes
- Use `assert_cmd` to spawn `/bin/echo` for shell tests; avoid sleep-based flakiness
- Env-scrub tests should set fake creds via `std::env::set_var` inside a `serial_test::serial` block

## Acceptance

- Every Phase 1 behavior bullet has at least one matching test
- `cargo test -p openclaudia` passes cleanly (no `#[ignore]` shortcuts)
- `cargo test --workspace -- --nocapture` shows the new tests by name
- No new clippy warnings (`cargo clippy --tests -- -D warnings`)
- Gap signals from Phase 1 confirmed filed as separate feature issues, not silently bundled here
